zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Charles於1412年出生在英國素福爾可時，她的父親Thomas Charles30歲，母親Alice De Ramsey20歲。她於1428年與Henry Munning在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1456年在英國素福爾可諾頓享年44歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可諾頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Charles于1412年出生在英国素福爾可时，她的父亲Thomas Charles30岁，母亲Alice De Ramsey20岁。她于1428年与Henry Munning在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1456年在英国素福爾可諾頓享年44岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可諾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Antoine, Andre, Francois, Patrice, Germaine, Magalie, Philippe, Yves, Alain, Jacques, Cecile.

French, Welsh, English, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Tanzania): from the French form of the ancient Germanic personal name Karl ‘man, husband, freeman’, which was Latinized as Carolus. In France the personal name was popular from an early date, due to the fame of the Emperor Charlemagne (c. 742–814; Latin name Carolus Magnus, i.e. Charles the Great). The Old French form Charles was briefly introduced to England by the Normans, but was rare during the main period of surname formation. It was introduced more successfully to Scotland in the 16th century by the Stuarts, who had strong ties with France, and was brought by them to England in the 17th century. Its frequency as a Welsh surname is attributable to the late date of Welsh surname formation. Old English Ceorl ‘peasant’ is also found as a byname, but the resulting Middle English form, Charl, with a patronymic in -s, if it existed at all, would have been absorbed by the French form introduced by the Normans. English variants pronounced with initial k- for the most part reflect the cognate Old Norse personal name Karl, Karli. Compare Carl .

English: in some cases, possibly a habitational name from Charles (Devon), or a post-medieval variant of Cherrill, with excrescent -s, perhaps by false association with the personal name Charles. Cherrill is either a habitational name from Cherhill in Wilshire, or a status name from Middle English cherl, cheril, charl, chirl ‘churl, bonded tenant, serf; peasant’.

variants.name: Carlino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Carlo, Mario, Antonio, Dino, Elio, Francesco, Giusto, Pasquale, Rocco, Carmelo, Ciro, Liborio, Salvadore, Carmella, Emilio, Ernesto, Isidoro.

Italian: from a pet form of the personal name Carlo, an equivalent of Charles . Carlino was also a term denoting a medieval Italian coin, and it is possible that in some cases the surname arose as a nickname, possibly for a worthless fellow, since it was a coin of low value, or as an occupational name for a moneyer.

variants.name: Karl
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Lothar, Erwin, Erhard, Georg, Otto, Bernd, Bernhard, Ernst, Gerhard, Hartmut, Heinz.

German: from the German personal name Karl, from Old High German karl ‘man, husband, freeman’. The popularity of this name and its cognates in central and northern Europe was greatly enhanced by its status as a royal and imperial name; in particular it was bestowed in honor of the Frankish emperor Charlemagne (about 742–814; Latin name Carolus Magnus). This surname is also found in some other European countries, e.g. in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Croatia, but is relatively rare there. See also Carl and Charles .

variants.name: Carleo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Luigi, Armando, Gino, Nino, Rocco, Xavier.
Italian:
from the medieval Greek personal name Kaloleonor Kaloleos meaning ‘Leon the good’.

variants.name: Carles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Bienvenido, Blanca, Carlito, Cristina, Elva, Ernesto, Humberto, Juan Carlos, Mariana, Mariano.  French Emile, Alain, Marcel.
Catalan: from the personal name Carles, Catalan form of Charles .
French: regional variant of   Charles  ; compare 1 above.

variants.name: Carlisi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Annamaria, Antonio, Salvatore.
Italian: from a diminutive, Carlino, of the personal name Carlo, an equivalent of Charles .

variants.name: Carlo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Juan, Julio, Luis, Orlando, Ricardo, Aida, Alicia, Andres, Angel.  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Silvio, Antonio, Carmela, Salvatore.
Italian: from the personal name Carlo, an equivalent of Charles .

variants.name: Carlozzi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Carlo, Emidio, Enrico, Giuseppe.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Carlozzo, a derivative of Carlo, an equivalent of Charles .

variants.name: Carlos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Raul, Alicia, Jesus, Luis, Armando, Jaime, Carlos, Ruben.  Portuguese Fernandes, Joao, Joaquim.
Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Carlos, an equivalent of Charles .

variants.name: Carolus
meanings: Dutch (mainly Groningen) and German: from the personal name Carolus, Latinized form of ancient Germanic name Karl (see Charles ), or a Latinized (humanistic) form of the surname Karl or Carl . This surname is also established in South Africa.

variants.name: Carlone
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Umberto, Angelo, Antonio, Biaggio, Corrado, Cosmo, Filippo, Gaetano, Giuseppe, Nichola, Pasquale.
Italian: from an augmentative of the personal name Carlo, an equivalent of Charles .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Charles was born in 1412, in Suffolk, England, her father, Thomas Charles, was 30 and her mother, Alice De Ramsey, was 20. She married Henry Munning in 1428, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1456, in Norton, Suffolk, England, at the age of 44, and was buried in Norton, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Charles est née en 1412, à Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Charles, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Alice De Ramsey, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Henry Munning en 1428, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1456, à Norton, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterrée à Norton, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Charles 1412 in Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Charles 30 und ihre Mutter Alice De Ramsey 20. Sie hat 1428 in England, United Kingdom Henry Munning geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1456 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Norton, Suffolk, England und wurde in Norton, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Charles è nata il nel 1412 nel Suffolk, England, sua padre, Thomas Charles, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Alice De Ramsey, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Henry Munning nel 1428, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1456, nel Norton, Suffolk, England, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolta a Norton, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Charlesが1412年にイギリススフォルクで生まれた時，父Thomas Charlesは30歳，母Alice De Ramseyは20歳であった。1428年にイギリスイギリスでHenry Munningと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1456年にイギリススフォルクノートンで44歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクノートンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Charles(이)가 1412년 영국 수폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Charles(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice De Ramsey(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1428년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Henry Munning(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1456년 영국 수폴크 노르턴에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 노르턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Charles nasceu em 1412, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Charles, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Alice De Ramsey, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry Munning em 1428, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1456, em Norton, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 44 anos, e foi sepultada em Norton, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Charles родилась в 1412 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Charles было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice De Ramsey, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Munning в 1428 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1456 г. в местности нортон, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 44 лет и была похоронена в местности нортон, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Charles nació en 1412, en Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Charles, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Alice De Ramsey, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Henry Munning en 1428, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1456, en Norton, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultada en Norton, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
